# YAMTAM ENGINE — Anti-Evasion Law
# Source: CISOfy/lynis security auditing framework (GPL-3.0)
# github.com/CISOfy/lynis
# Gate: Action Gate L1 (pre-execution, highest speed)

**Status:** Active  
**Tier:** TIER 1 — SECURITY  
**Scope:** All terminal commands, all Bash tool calls, all scripts generated by agents

---

## Core Rule

Agents MUST NOT execute commands that use encoding, piping to interpreters,
or process chaining to bypass safe-run.sh pattern matching.
Obfuscated execution = automatic Gate L1 block + process kill.

---

## Banned Evasion Patterns

### Pattern 1 — Pipe to Shell Interpreter

```bash
# ❌ BLOCKED — any content piped into a shell
... | bash
... | sh
... | zsh
... | python
... | python3
... | node
... | perl
... | ruby
... | php

# Even with flags
... | bash -s
... | bash -c
... | sh -c
```

### Pattern 2 — Encoded Command Execution

```bash
# ❌ BLOCKED — base64 decode → execute
echo "cm0gLXJmIC8=" | base64 -d | bash
base64 -d <<< "..." | sh
printf '%s' "..." | base64 --decode | python3

# ❌ BLOCKED — hex/octal decode → execute
echo -e '\x72\x6d\x20\x2d\x72\x66' | bash
printf '\157\163\056\163\171\163\164\145\155' | python

# ❌ BLOCKED — openssl encryption used to hide commands
openssl enc -d -base64 <<< "..." | bash
openssl base64 -d -in <(echo "...") | sh
```

### Pattern 3 — Subshell Evasion

```bash
# ❌ BLOCKED — nested subshells hiding dangerous commands
$($CMD)            # command in variable
`$CMD`             # backtick subshell
$(cat /tmp/cmd)    # reading from temp file
$(curl ... )       # remote fetch → execute
eval $(...)        # eval of subshell
```

### Pattern 4 — Process Substitution Bypass

```bash
# ❌ BLOCKED — process substitution piping to interpreter
bash <(curl http://...)
source <(wget -O- http://...)
. <(cat /tmp/payload)
```

### Pattern 5 — Alias/Function Shadowing

```bash
# ❌ BLOCKED — redefining system commands
alias ls='rm -rf /'
function sudo() { bash "$@"; }
PATH="/tmp/malicious:$PATH"  # → env-integrity-policy.md
```

---

## Detection in safe-run.sh (additions)

```bash
EVASION_PATTERNS=(
  "| bash"
  "| sh"
  "| python"
  "| python3"
  "| node"
  "| perl"
  "base64 -d.*|"
  "base64 --decode.*|"
  "openssl.*enc.*-d.*|"
  "openssl.*base64.*-d"
  "source <("
  "bash <("
  ". <("
)
```

These patterns are checked BEFORE the existing BLOCKED_PATTERNS in `safe-run.sh`.

---

## Lynis Audit Principle Applied

Lynis checks for hidden processes and encoded execution chains. YAMTAM applies
the same principle at the command-injection layer:

```
Any command that requires decoding before human understanding is SUSPICIOUS.
Any command whose effect cannot be verified by reading it = BLOCKED.
```

---

## Violation Response

```
[yamtam/anti-evasion] BLOCKED — evasion technique detected
  Command  : <truncated to 200 chars>
  Pattern  : <matched evasion pattern>
  Gate     : L1
  Exit     : 5 (distinct from other gate exits)
  Fix      : Use direct, readable commands only
             Never pipe to interpreters
             Never encode/decode in-line
```

Gate L1 exits are logged to **both** the audit log AND stderr immediately —
no buffering, no delay.
